REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS IS OFF CALENDAR. THIS STATUTORY MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS WAS SET TO BE MADE TO THE COURT WITHIN 30 DAYS OF THE ORIGINAL TRIAL DATE. ACCORDING TO THE NOTICE OF MOTION, THIS WAS SOLELY A STATUTORY MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS WITH NO REFERENCE TO THE COMMON LAW IN THE NOTICE. FURTHERMORE, PARTIES FAILED TO MEET AND CONFER IN-PERSON, VIA TELEPHONE OR VIDEO CONFERENCE. PLAINTIFF MAY FILE A NON-STATUTORY MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS IF NECESSARY AND PROPER AND SHALL ADDRESS SPECIFICALLY THE PURPORTED DEFECTS IN THE ANSWER. PLAINTIFF'S GENERAL STATEMENTS WITHOUT REFERENCED TO ANY PARTICULAR AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES OR THEIR ELEMENTS IN THE POINTS AND AUTHORITIES ARE NOT SUFFICIENT TO CARRY THE BURDEN. (E.G. "HERE, DEFENDANT'S ANSWER CONTAINS BOILERPLATE, REPETITIVE AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES RIFE WITH ERRORS, AND NONE OF THEM ARE EVEN AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES AT ALL. FURTHERMORE, NONE OF DEFENDANT'S ASSERTED DEFENSES CONTAIN ANY FACTUAL ALLEGATIONS TO SUPPORT THEM. (SEE RJN, EX. A, PP. 2-5.)" POINTS AND AUTHORITIES 7:17-19). JUDGE: CHARLES F, HAINES ; CLERK: L. YOUNG ; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS IS DENIED. DEFENDANT'S VERIFIED ANSWER FOLLOWS, PARAGRAPH BY PARAGRAPH, PLAINTIFF'S 395 PARAGRAPH THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT AND EITHER DENIES, ADMITS, OR DENIES BASED ON LACK OF INFORMATION OR BELIEF, EACH AND EVERY ALLEGATION. (CODE CIV. PROC., SECTION 431.30, SUBD. (D).) THE DENIAL IN THE ANSWER PUTS THE ALLEGATIONS DIRECTLY AT ISSUE AND MAKES IT NECESSARY FOR THE PLAINTIFF TO INTRODUCE EVIDENCE THEREOF BEFORE HE IS ENTITLED TO JUDGMENT." (CUNEO V. LAWSON (1928) 203 CAL. 190, 193-194; SEE ALSO, BARASCH V. EPSTEIN (1957) 147 CAL.APP.2D 439, 443 ["THE DENIALS OF THE ANSWER ARE SUFFICIENT TO, AND DO, CREATE ISSUES. PROOF OF THE ALLEGATIONS OF THE COMPLAINT, WHICH ARE DENIED, IS NECESSARY BEFORE PLAINTIFF MAY RECOVER JUDGMENT. SINCE THE ANSWER ... DENIES MATERIAL ALLEGATIONS OF THE COMPLAINT, IT IS SUFFICIENT AS AGAINST A GENERAL DEMURRER AND PLAINTIFF WAS NOT ENTITLED TO JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS."].) WHILE THE COURT AGREES THAT DEFENDANT'S AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES ARE NOT WELL PLED AND CONSIST OF LEGAL CONCLUSIONS THIS DOES NOT UNDERCUT THE FACT THAT EACH MATERIAL FACT IN THE COMPLAINT HAS BEEN DENIED AND PUT AT ISSUE BY THE DENIAL AND THEREFORE PLAINTIFF IS NOT ENTITLED TO JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS. is DENIED. Motion for summary judgment/adjudication is framed by the pleadings. Operative complaint in this action is the Third Amended Complaint filed and served on June 2, 2023. Answer filed on October 2, 2023 (Note: Motion to Vacate Default as to Plaintiff's Third Amended Verified Complaint filed on August 18, 2023). MOTION TO QUASH SUBPOENAS
Off Calendar Feb-05-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Wayne Quint, Esq. 910-478-5295 The Quint Firm for Plaintiff.
Cassandra Francois, Esq. 408-288-3868 Williams Pinelli & Cullen for Defendant Rosenthal et al.
John Demarest, Esq. 818-206-0220 Hanger Steinberg Shapiro & Ash for Defendant.

Parties state appearance. The Court announces the tentative ruling. The Court states a timely notice of contest the TR was received.

ARGUED. Ms. Francois does not dispute the Court's TR. Counsel verbally objects to 2nd supplemental submitted by Plaintiff this morning. The Court strikes the 2nd supplemental as untimely. Mr. Quint contests and argues, presents meet and confer timeline, fixed problems, party has a fee waiver, disproportionate sanction. Court comments, 16 discovery motions, poses questions, admonishes parties, states civil discvoery act. Ms Francois' brief rebuttal; failure to meet and confer, retaliatory, sanctions warranted. Mr. Demarest requests audio recording of meet and confer. The Court grants the recording of the meet and confer, parties to submit transcripts to the court if necessary, moving party to pay for transcripts or the court may shift costs.

The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

Defendant Deborah Rosenthal's motion to quash subpoenas.

Taken Under Submission.***

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

Notice Of And Motion For Terminating, Issue, Evidence, And/Or Monetary Sanctions Of $2,161.65 As To Plaintiff Loran Simon For Failure To Obey Court Order Re: Responses To Discovery; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Request For Judicial Notice; [Proposed] Order; Declaration Of Josephine Baurac Filed Concurrently
Off Calendar Jan-05-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT 426 FILLMORE ASSOCATION's Motion For Terminating, Issue, Evidence, And/Or Monetary Sanctions Of $2,161.65 As To Plaintiff Loran Simon For Failure To Obey Court Order Re: Responses To Discovery.

1 - Defendant 426 Fillmore Association's ("HOA") motion for terminating, issue, evidence, and/or monetary sanctions is DENIED.

Defendant HOA argues that Plaintiff Loran Simon willfully violated this court's 8/22/25 orders. The record demonstrates Plaintiff appeared at the 8/22/25 hearing and had actual notice of the court's 8/22/2025 rulings (adopting its tentative rulings) that discovery responses must be provided by 9/12/25. In addition, on October 16, 2025, Defendant HOA informed Plaintiff of his noncompliance and sought the responses. (Baurac Decl, Ex. D.) Plaintiff failed to respond to this communication. (Baurac Decl., par. 14.) The record, however, demonstrates that Defendant HOA never served notice of those orders on Plaintiff in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure section1019.5. The record does not demonstrate Plaintiff waived notice. (See Simon Decl., par. 17.) Given Defendants' failure to comply with the notice requirement, the court declines to award terminating, issue, or evidence sanctions.

The court orders Plaintiff to respond to the discovery in compliance with the 8/22/25 orders no later than January 26, 2026. Plaintiff contends the underlying discovery motions were untimely, but the court will not revisit the prior rulings, which rejected this contention.

Sanctions are denied.

Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

Defendant 426 Filmore's motion to compel plaintiff's further responses to its set one document requests is granted. Plaintiff must serve verified code-compliant further responses to 426 Fillmore's first set of document requests, produce all non-privileged responsive documents, and serve a privilege log listing all privileged responsive documents that were not produced no later than September 12, 2025. Plaintiff is required to respond to this discovery notwithstanding that he is not represented by counsel and that this discovery may be similar to discovery propounded by other other defendants. DISCOVERY
Off Calendar Aug-11-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF LORAN SIMOn's Motion For Reconsideration Of June 5, 2025 Order Re: Plaintiff'S Motion To Compel Defendant 426 Fillmore Association'S Further Responses To Form Interrogatories.

Plaintiff Loren Simon's Motion For Reconsideration Of June 5, 2025, Order Re: Plaintiff's Motion To Compel Defendant 426 Fillmore Association's Further Responses To Form Interrogatories is DENIED.

Plaintiff moves under Code of Civil Procedure section 1008, subdivisions (a) and (b). Under both subdivisions, the reconsideration motion must be "based upon new or different facts, circumstances, or law." (Code of Civ. Proc. 1008(a)&(b).) The moving party bears the burden of proving that the alleged "new or different facts, circumstances, or law" could not, with reasonable diligence, have been discovered or produced earlier. (New York Times Co. v. Superior Court (2005) 135 Cal.App.4th 206, 212-213.) This standard is jurisdictional. (See Code of Civil Procedure section 1008(e).)

Plaintiff here has not demonstrated any new facts, law or circumstances justifying the requested relief. Specifically, the court finds the Notice of Motion for Injunctive Relief, filed February 8, 2024, is not new and Plaintiff failed to demonstrate he could not have with reasonable diligence produced it with his discovery motion. Nor does Plaintiff identify any other fact or authority that could not have with reasonable diligence been produced during the proceedings on the motion.

As brought under Code of Civil Procedure section 1008, subdivisions (a) and (b), the motion is denied.

The court regrets the typographical errors in the June 5, 2025, Order, but the substance of the order is clear and unaffected by the errors belatedly identified by Plaintiff.

The court declines to exercise its authority to reconsider its June 5, 2025, Order on its own motion.

Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

Plaintiff's Motion to Compel Defendant 426 Fillmore Association's ("HOA") Further Amended Discovery Responses to Plaintiff's Request For Production of Documents, Set No. Two, And Request For Sanctions is GRANTED.

Notice And Motion To Compel Defendant 426 Fillmore Associations Further Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set No. One, And Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jun-05-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

2 - Plaintiff's motion to compel further responses from Defendant 426 Fillmore Association to form interrogatories is GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART.

Plaintiff moves under Code of Civil Procedure section 2030.300. "On receipt of a response to interrogatories, the propounding party may move for an order compelling a further response if the propounding party deems that any of the following apply: (1) An answer to a particular interrogatory is evasive or incomplete. (2) An exercise of the option to produce documents under Section 2030.230 is unwarranted or the required specification of those documents is inadequate. (3) An objection to an interrogatory is without merit or too general." (Code of Civil Procedure section 2030.300(a).)

Plaintiff demonstrated reasonable meet and confer.

The special interrogatories at issue are reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. (See Code of Civil Procedure Section 2017.010.)

The court compels further response to form interrogatories nos. 1.1, 13.1, 16.1, 16.2. 16.9 &16.10. Plaintiff has demonstrated Defendant's response to these interrogatories was incomplete and evasive. Defendant has not demonstrated the merit of any objections. The balance tips in favor of the discovery at issue. (See Parris v. Superior Court (2003) 109 Cal.App.4th 285, 300-301.) Plaintiff seeks routine and appropriate discovery for a wage and hour action such as this one that is appropriately calculated to discover foundational information regarding the contours of the class; Plaintiff appears to be a member of the class she seeks to represent and Defendant's contentions otherwise are conjectural. Production of the discovery at issue is favored, and here production will likely help class members preserve their rights. Production at this time serves interest of judicial efficiency and economy. The risk of abuse of the information sought is non-existent as to most of it; any risk that does exist can readily be managed with an appropriate protective order-likely a t most a modification of the order the parties have already agreed to.

Defendant must provide supplemental responses by June 30, 2025.

The court declines compels further response to form interrogatories nos. 12.4, 12.5, 12.6, 12.7, 15.1, 16.3, 16.4, 16.5, 16.6, 16.7, 16.8, 17.1, 50.1, 50.2, 50.3, 50.4, 50.5 & 50.6,. Plaintiff has not demonstrated Defendant's response to these interrogatories was incomplete and evasive.

Sanctions requests are denied.

Plaintiff's request for judicial notice is granted.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and must drop a hard courtesy copy in Dept. 302's inbox.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: M. Macadangdang; Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

Notice And Motion To Compel Defendant 426 Fillmore Associations Further Responses To Plaintiffs Requests For Admission, Set One, And Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jun-05-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

1 - Plaintiff's motion to compel further response Defendant 426 Fillmore Association to requests for admission is DENIED.

Answers to RFAs are to be "as complete and straightforward as the information reasonably available. . . permits." (Code of Civil Procedure section 2033.220(a).) When the response is an answer to the substance of the request, the answer must: (1) Admit so much of the matter involved in the request as is true, either as expressed in the request itself or as reasonably and clearly qualified by the responding party. (2) Deny so much of the matter involved in the request as is untrue. (3) Specify so much of the matter involved in the request as to the truth of which the responding party lacks sufficient information or knowledge. (Code of Civil Procedure section 2033.220(b).) "On receipt of a response to requests for admissions, the party requesting admissions may move for an order compelling a further response if that party deems that either or both of the following apply: (1) An answer to a particular request is evasive or incomplete. (2) An objection to a particular request is without merit or too general." (Code of Civil Procedure section 2033.290(a).)

The following RFAs are not proper and, therefore, the court will not compel further responses: ## 1, 2. Requests to "admit" legal conclusions are not proper.

Defendant's responses to the following RFAs are Code-compliant: ## 3, 4, 5. 6, 7, 8. 9, 10, 11.

Defendant's objection to the Request for Genuineness #1 is sustained; Plaintiff did not demonstrate compliance with Code of Civil Procedure section 2033.060(g).)

Plaintiff's requests for sanctions is denied.

Plaintiff's request for judicial notice is granted.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and must drop a hard courtesy copy in Dept. 302's inbox.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: M. Macadangdang; Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS
Off Calendar May-28-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

PLAINTIFF LORAN SIMON MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS is DENIED. Defendant's verified Answer follows, paragraph by paragraph, Plaintiff's 395 paragraph Third Amended Complaint and either denies, admits, or denies based on lack of information or belief, each and every allegation. (Code Civ. Proc., section 431.30, subd. (d).) The denial in the answer puts the allegations directly at issue and makes it necessary for the plaintiff to introduce evidence thereof before he is entitled to judgment." (Cuneo v. Lawson (1928) 203 Cal. 190, 193-194; see also, Barasch v. Epstein (1957) 147 Cal.App.2d 439, 443 ["The denials of the answer are sufficient to, and do, create issues. Proof of the allegations of the complaint, which are denied, is necessary before plaintiff may recover judgment. Since the answer ... denies material allegations of the complaint, it is sufficient as against a general demurrer and plaintiff was not entitled to judgment on the pleadings."].) While the Court agrees that Defendant's affirmative defenses are not well pled and consist of legal conclusions this does not undercut the fact that each material fact in the complaint has been denied and put at issue by the denial and therefore Plaintiff is not entitled to judgment on the pleadings.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b)
Judge: Victor M. Hwang ; Clerk: L. Young ; Not Reported. (D501)

PLAINTIFF LORAN SIMON MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS is OFF CALENDAR. This statutory motion for judgment on the pleadings was set to be made to the Court within 30 days of the original trial date. According to the Notice of Motion, this was solely a statutory motion for judgment on the pleadings with no reference to the common law in the Notice. Plaintiff contests the court's tentative ruling. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the Court rules as follows:

DEFENDANTS TODD BRABEC, NANCY GOLDSEN AND DEBORAH ROSENTHAL'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part. Demurrer to the thirteenth cause of action is also overruled. All elements are sufficiently alleged.
